hypothesis: The current sustained chord bed is loud enough but too bright, too mono, and pumps at the chord change, so this pass darkens both locked layers, shifts body toward the centered electric-piano fundamental, smooths the 2.6s gain hump, and widens only the string ensemble with shallow 3.8 Hz motion.

operations.change: lower starting cutoff for dark fundamental body
operations.reason: Critic reports candidate centroid starts far above target and excess mid/presence/air.

operations.change: lower ending cutoff so tone darkens over time
operations.reason: Reference centroid falls through the clip while render gets brighter.

operations.change: replace cutoff automation with downward darkening curve
operations.reason: Targets the wrong upward brightness motion without changing MIDI.

operations.change: reduce resonance for smoother low organ tone
operations.reason: Keeps the dark layer from adding nasal/presence peaks.

operations.change: increase controlled sub/fundamental support
operations.reason: Target is bass-dominant and chroma is correct, so body should come from source support not note edits.

operations.change: reduce buzzy square-saw blend
operations.reason: Critic asks for sine/triangle plus low square character instead of saw-heavy upper partials.

operations.change: move wavetable toward a purer darker organ body
operations.reason: Reduces upper-harmonic density while retaining the sustained chord source.

operations.change: narrow and center the fundamental layer
operations.reason: Keeps bass/body stable while strings provide side energy.

operations.change: reduce synth spread on the body layer
operations.reason: Prevents widened low fundamentals from destabilizing the center.

operations.change: lower body layer base gain after adding sub support
operations.reason: Full RMS is already close and added sub needs headroom.

operations.change: smooth gain automation and reduce the 2.6s lift
operations.reason: Weak windows show a large RMS overshoot at 2.62-2.75s while final sustain still needs gradual support.

operations.change: cut high band on the body layer
operations.reason: Render has far too much presence/air relative to bass.

operations.change: trim mid buildup on the body layer
operations.reason: Global band balance shows excess mid energy versus the target.

operations.change: lower starting cutoff for dark ensemble
operations.reason: Strings are acting as a bright saw pad while target is a dark organ/string bed.

operations.change: lower ending cutoff for darker final sustain
operations.reason: Reference becomes darker by the end and final window is still proportionally too bright.

operations.change: replace string cutoff with downward darkening curve
operations.reason: Pulls spectral centroid down over the same locked chord timing.

operations.change: soften filter resonance
operations.reason: Avoids nasal presence while lowering cutoff.

operations.change: darken saw-stack position
operations.reason: Reduces bright saw partial density but keeps ensemble identity.

operations.change: reduce bright string blend
operations.reason: Moves the string pad toward softer ensemble body.

operations.change: widen string layer
operations.reason: Target width and side energy are much higher while the body layer remains centered.

operations.change: keep maximum string stereo spread
operations.reason: Builds width from chorus/unison instead of long reverb.

operations.change: keep moderate chorus ensemble width
operations.reason: Adds side energy without increasing reverb tail or blurring 50ms envelope.

operations.change: reduce to shallow fast modulation
operations.reason: Reference motion is around 3.8 Hz with shallow depth; prior motion reads too deep.

operations.change: smooth string gain automation around chord change
operations.reason: Reduces the local 2.6-2.8s hump while preserving a gradual late sustain lift.

operations.change: cut high/presence fizz on strings
operations.reason: Render has excess air and presence that the target nearly lacks.

operations.change: trim string mid glare
operations.reason: Helps shift the global balance toward bass and low-mid.

operations.change: add modest low-mid body to strings
operations.reason: Supports the target bass/low-mid weighting without increasing brightness.

operations.change: soften body-layer attack
operations.reason: Opening and chord-change windows are still too loud in 50ms scoring; slower attack smooths the locked note onsets without retiming notes.

operations.change: soften string attack
operations.reason: Reduces the 2.6s chord-change hump and opening transient while preserving sustained string width.

operations.change: restore more late body after smoothing chord change
operations.reason: The v2 candidate fixed the 2.6s overshoot but ended below the target RMS, so the final lift is increased gradually without adding brightness.

operations.change: restore more late string sustain after smoothing chord change
operations.reason: The ending window improved spectrally but became too quiet; this preserves the dark final sustain with a smooth late ramp.

loss_trials.notes: Rejected v1: full score improved to 0.321 and spectral_timbre 0.412, centroid_trajectory 0.310, stereo_width 0.691 improved, but exact_envelope_50ms fell to 0.385 and sustain_shape fell to 0.649; local attacks still too hot.

Rejected v2: slower attacks improved opening/chord-change local windows versus v1, but full score dropped to 0.300 and exact_envelope_50ms remained below current at 0.405; kept the slower attack idea for final candidate because it supports the envelope objective.

Accepted tradeoff for this envelope/timbre pass: full final 0.301 vs current 0.294, spectral_timbre 0.410 vs 0.264, spectral_features 0.213 vs 0.010, centroid_trajectory 0.299 vs 0.015, harmonic_noise 0.414 vs 0.370, modulation 0.304 vs 0.289, stereo_width 0.692 vs 0.670. exact_envelope_50ms and sustain_shape remain worse globally, but targeted opening and chord-change windows improved.
Opening window improved over current: final 0.436 vs 0.284, exact_envelope_50ms 0.164 vs 0.037, spectral_timbre 0.592 vs 0.294, centroid much closer; sustain_shape slightly lower.

Chord-change window improved over current: final 0.471 vs 0.347, exact_envelope_50ms 0.241 vs 0.139, spectral_timbre 0.542 vs 0.260, centroid end 306 Hz vs current 728 Hz; sustain_shape lower but RMS overshoot reduced.

Ending window improves final 0.535 vs current 0.468 and spectral_timbre 0.561 vs 0.315 with sustain_shape held at 1.0; exact_envelope_50ms is slightly lower at 0.477 vs 0.501 because the darkened ending is quieter than current but less bright.
